method_atom: A procedure for building multiplicative characters (ring-valued functionals) on a Hopf algebra of combinatorial objects so that the functional is compatible with the algebra's recursive coproduct decomposition, and factors through a Grothendieck-type ring of equivalence classes of geometric spaces. Each object is assigned an invariant computed from the class of the complement of an associated algebraic variety, or from characteristic classes of singular varieties, with a formula expressing the invariant of a composite (a join of two spaces) in terms of the components, and recovering an Euler-characteristic-type topological quantity as a special value.
object_atom: Feynman graphs of perturbative quantum field theory and their associated affine and projective graph hypersurfaces, together with the BPHZ renormalization procedure and partition functions built from them.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the Hopf-algebra-character-through-a-Grothendieck-ring valuation of combinatorial structures via variety-complement and characteristic-class invariants; the domain-specific object is Feynman graphs, their hypersurfaces, and renormalization in QFT, which the graph-Hopf-algebra structure makes partly inseparable from the method.
